feedback.text: As you might know, Stop Killing Games (read, remove games you buy from your game inventory after you buy it) initiative is on the run, to fight of capitalist scum from burning down people's properties. What is happening, is that game companies have "the legal right" to "remove things you buy from them" from your possession, retroactive or future actively. It is akin to an author writing a book, selling millions of copies, and then remotely activating a chip in them to burn them all down because they don't want to be an author anymore. it would, in every other situation, be illegal. This is a modern world; there are alternatives. You can kill your games from active servers while gifting the code to public so that people can -at the very least- run things locally or otherwise support the game they love to keep it playable outside of the gaming company's grasp. It is what the gamer heart desires, and this could in fact affect EVERY SINGLE GAME in the future. We are talking from wacraft3 to every triple A game with online must-use features. Why does a game company have different rights to every other company on the planet? Why are they not legally binded to make their product backwards compatible with systems others can adopt? It is greed, and greed only. I want to own the game I buy. I want to pass it to my kids.
